授業目的: 1. To get an overview of Japan's history until 1640.2. To learn how to work with sources.3. To polish the academic skills of the participants.
到達目標: 1. Participants have a grasp of Japan's history until 1640.  They can identify important events and persons, and explain the historical contexts, as well as discuss how the importance of those particular details is argued.2. Participants can analyze a source [in translation] and contextualize it in the framework of history studied in class.3. Participants can produce an academic discussion of an academic article on a subject related to the course topic.
特記事項: 【合併】総合日本学習科目（歴史Ａ）（CIEC）/Japanese History A（国際学部）
授業方法: Lectures based on discussions of the textbook, analyses of source materials and selected academic articles.  Taking turns students will be responsible for starting off the discussion of the textbook.  Online tests will keep you on track of your studies.  Regularly, but not in every class, participants must submit answers, questions and comments related to material discussed in class.

第1回:
【Startup Week】  URL:https://～・・・
"Online class（On-demand format）"／ 《90minutes》
Introduction: a general overview of the period we will cover. Practical information. A first source analysis exercise is included in the video. You will get a feedback in the next class.
For next week: Get a copy of the textbook and familiarize yourself with it. The first chapter is available on LUNA. Read it for next week.
第2回:
【Core Weeks①】"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
Japan before 600 Read chapter 1.
What is the structure of the chapter, and what are its learning points?
Take quiz 1. Read Chapter 2 "An end to growth, 600-800." Make a scheme of the learning points in the chapter. This will help you answer the questions in the quiz.
第3回:
【Core Weeks②】"Face-to-face class" 《90minutes》
The Taika Reform and Nara.

Take quiz 2. Assignment of Heian literature. Read one of four texts from the Heian age. Write a report of your findings, cf. instructions given in class. Prepare to discuss your findings in a group. For next week Read Chapter 3.
第4回: 【Flex Hours】10 October, Saturday. 9-16. Field study in Osaka that will focus on the history of the city and give you a sense of the relevance of its geography. We meet at Umeda Station at 9 a.m. The first stop is the Osaka City Museum of History.  After lunch in the neighborhood, we go south to Sumiyoshi Taisha, before taking the tram to Shitennoji Temple. From the temple we will walk to Nanba via the Shinsekai area.  《180minutes》
Take the quiz at the museum, and submit a field study report on LUNA cf. the guidelines provided.
第5回:
【Core Weeks③】"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
Nara age Japan.
第6回:
【Core Weeks④】"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
Heian. Chapter 3
第7回:
【Core Weeks⑤】"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
Group discussions of Heian literature. There will be two steps. First, discuss your findings with peers who have read the same selection as youself. Second, discuss your findings with peers who have other selections. Listen and take notes so you can write a summary of the second discussion.
Write and submit a summary of your discussion in class together with your report. The deadline is next Wednesday.  Read Read Chapter 4.
第8回:
【Core Weeks⑥】"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
Decline of the Heian structures
Take quiz 4. For next week Read Chapter 5.
第9回:
【Core Weeks⑦】"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
Kamakura: Economics and Political Tensions in an Epoch of Minimal Growth.
Take quiz 5. For next week Read Chapter 6.
第10回:
【Core Weeks⑧】"Face-to-face class" 《90minutes》
Muromachi era.“Medieval”culture.
第11回:
【Core Weeks➈】"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
Warring states
第12回:
【Core Weeks⑩】"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
Christian Century; Tokugawa Era
第13回:
【Core Weeks⑪】"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
Unification under Tokugawa
第14回:
【Core Weeks⑫】"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
 Exam that includes questions from the online quizzes and work with a source. The only tools permitted are pencils and erasers.
トピック.授業外学習2: Please, buy the course book in advance.  Expect about 4 hours of home work per week including the time for taking the online quizzes.  The course is given in English.

教科書/Required texts0: 1. W.W. Farris "Japan to 1600" (University of Hawai'i Press, 2009)
学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: 各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する能力が養成されます。各学部・研究科のDP、CPや教育課程表（授業科目一覧等）は、下記リンクこちらに掲載していますので学修計画の参考にしてください。https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1※kwicへのリンクとなります（在学生のみ閲覧可）
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading2: Field study report 20% and Heian literature report 20%,
成績評価Grading3: 授業外学修課題／Assignment, homework required outside class (06)
成績評価Grading4: 授業への参加度（自発性、積極性、主体性、等）／In-class participation,contribution (09)
More than 3 absences from the core weeks = fail.
成績評価Grading5: 備考　Note The regular quizzes count 20 percent and you must have completed and passed 80 percent of them to get a passable credit. NB: Presence and active participation in class is expected. Copy-past is illegal, and if found in any written assignment you will fail the class. If you use GenAI for solving questions or assignments, treat the result as a quotation. Identify the program you used (name and version), and include a list of the prompt(s) you used. To pass, the total amount quotations should not exceed 20 percent of the text you submit.
備考Note0: You are welcome to contact me at christian.hermansen@kwansei.ac.jp
